next_step_text: 15th of june 26 - Confidence : 10% Ask Bihag if he will ask for approval of this project again in September 14th of April 26 - Confidence : 10% BIhag Shah (CPO) called me back : Due to Contract Management project being late SGD to be sold this year or 27 by PAI Partners => No budget for S2P in 26, Bihag mentioned to me that he will do his best to have this project budgeted for 27 so I postponed th

buyer_dependent_items.text: Confirm whether SGD will budget the S2P project (Bihag's September approval attempt) and share PAI Partners' direction

competitive_position.summary: Zycus was shortlisted Jan 2026 to two finalists but lost the standalone CLM scope 6 Feb 2026 to an unnamed niche CLM player (~100k cheaper over 3 years, ~98 percent fit vs ~90 percent, higher-rated AI per Legal). The remaining S2P scope kept Zycus as a finalist against one other unnamed S2P supplier, with IT (per Pierre) leaning to SAP/Ariba though the buyer did not confirm it. The named Competitors__c rivals (Coupa, GEP, iValua, JAGGAER) never appeared in any call and are dormant. Recency-weighted, the CLM niche player is the strongest current threat (won CLM Feb 2026), but the deal's decisive blocker is not a competitor, it is the PE owner's frozen budget. No new call evidence this run, so threat levels are carried forward.

competitors.name: CLM niche player (unnamed)
competitors.quote: Bihag called me for CLM they selected a niche player (100K less expensive over 3 years) / Bihag will communicate me the name when it will be finalized ... competition (unknown CLM provider, preferred by Legal - Better AI features)
competitors.source: SF Next_Step__c 6 Feb 2026, 2 Feb 2026; Avoma 'Final Sales negotiation SGD' 29 Jan 2026, Bihag Shah
competitors.how_we_win: Lost CLM on price and AI. For S2P re-engagement lead with the Merlin agentic AI story Legal wanted but could not get standalone, and quantify the single-platform integration tax versus running CLM, sourcing, and eProcurement as three separate vendors.

competitors.name: Second S2P finalist (unnamed)
competitors.quote: for the other modules, I'm negotiating with someone ... the second supplier whom we have finally listed there, we have not done yet this exercise with them like what we have done for you because they are not selected for CLM
competitors.source: Avoma 'Final Sales negotiation SGD' 29 Jan 2026, Bihag Shah; SF Next_Step__c 27 Feb 2026 ('two remaining vendors')
competitors.how_we_win: Zycus is the only finalist that ran the full IT integration exercise; make that head-start and the CFO's in-principle S2P agreement the case for staying sole-negotiated rather than reopening an S2P RFP.

competitors.name: SAP / Ariba
competitors.quote: I've clearly the feeling that IT is not for us ... I've clearly the feeling that IT is for iAriba or SAP ... most of the time IT is defending SAP because they prefer a single solution
competitors.source: Avoma 'Final Sales negotiation SGD' 29 Jan 2026, Pierre Meraud (rep observation, not buyer-confirmed)
competitors.how_we_win: Rep-perceived IT lean, not a stated buyer preference; neutralize by winning the SAP S4HANA on-prem and Salesforce integration proof already validated in the 28 Jan technical session.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: Named in SF Competitors__c; no mention in any Avoma call or Next_Step since Jun 2025.
competitors.how_we_win: Not an active threat; monitor only if the buyer reopens an S2P RFP in 2027.

competitors.name: GEP Worldwide
competitors.quote: Named in SF Competitors__c; no mention in any Avoma call or Next_Step since Jun 2025.
competitors.how_we_win: Not an active threat; monitor only if the buyer reopens an S2P RFP in 2027.

competitors.quote: Named in SF Competitors__c; no mention in any Avoma call or Next_Step since Jun 2025.
competitors.how_we_win: Not an active threat; monitor only if the buyer reopens an S2P RFP in 2027.

champion_strength.source: Avoma 29 Jan 2026; Next_Step__c 2 Feb 2026, 27 Feb 2026, 14 Apr 2026; Task 2026-05-27
champion_strength.summary: Bihag Shah (Group CPO) is a strong, engaged champion with sustained 12-month advocacy, direct CFO access, and proven ability to drive internal consensus and keep Zycus the only S2P finalist. He is not cooling by choice, the stall is external (PE-owner budget freeze). Current risk is capacity: since his 27 May 2026 defer he has gone quiet, and if PAI Partners declines 2027 budget he cannot advance S2P regardless of advocacy. What is still missing is a second executive thread that survives independently of him.

sources: Avoma 'Final Sales negotiation SGD' 29 Jan 2026, Bihag Shah
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: Philippe Pourquery (Group CFO, who also heads IT) is the internal economic buyer: he approved CLM budget in 2025, held in-principle agreement on S2P in January 2026, and was set to arbitrate the S2P commitment with Bihag on 30 Jan 2026 at 11:30 (source: Next_Step__c 2 Feb 2026, Avoma 29 Jan 2026). The decisive authority, however, sits ABOVE the CFO: private-equity owner PAI Partners must approve S2P because SGD is under an LBO and PAI is weighing a sale of the company. Bihag on 29 Jan 2026: 'we have all agreements internally is done, but we do not have official go for all our IT projects yet, it is still pending at our shareholders level.' The EB is mapped and positive, but the real budget gate is the PE owner, whom Zycus has never reached.

recommended_moves: Send Bihag a French S2P reference proof point to hold the relationship warm over summer.
Build the single-platform integration-tax and Merlin AI business case for Bihag's September budget push.
Keep the CFO (Philippe Pourquery) and Fiona threads alive with a light value update so a restart does not depend only on Bihag.
